rules.rule: Contributors must fork the target repository before contributing — never push feature branches directly to upstream.
After forking, clone the personal fork to a local machine before creating branches.
Branches must be pushed to the personal fork — never to the official/upstream repository.
Branch names must follow the pattern: users/[username]/[category]-[entity]-[action].
The [username] segment must be the contributor's GitHub username.
The [category] segment must be taken from the approved category list.
The [entity] segment must identify the domain entity the work concerns.
The [action] segment must describe the action being performed.
All segments must be lowercase and hyphen-separated.
Each branch must be focused on a single concern.
Must not commit directly to main.
Must not use generic branch names such as fix, test, temp, or wip.
Must not reuse old branch names for new work.
Remote branches should be deleted after merging.
